use_policy.authority_boundary: This model describes exposure and countercontrols; it neither authorizes data collection nor states that any provider performs the modeled acts.

asset_planes.name: Operational and process ontology
asset_planes.definition: Workflow states, roles, queues, systems, task taxonomies, templates, approvals, economics, exceptions, and handoffs.
asset_planes.boundary: Process structure can be sensitive even when no document text is present; metadata combinations can identify matters or institutional priorities.

asset_planes.name: Professional judgment ontology
asset_planes.definition: Issue framing, choices, risk weighting, fallbacks, escalation thresholds, edits, rationales, negotiation posture, and quality standards.
asset_planes.boundary: This is an operational representation of judgment, not a copy of a lawyer's mind and not proof that a choice was legally correct.

asset_planes.name: Client and matter content
asset_planes.definition: Identities, facts, communications, evidence, drafts, objectives, advice, personal data, financial information, and prospective-client information.
asset_planes.boundary: Information relating to a representation is broader than attorney-client privilege; privilege, work product, confidentiality, privacy, and trade secret rules remain distinct.

asset_planes.name: Public-derived legal evidence
asset_planes.definition: Dockets, filed documents, opinions, public deal and regulatory documents, biographies, and authorized public statements.
asset_planes.boundary: Public outputs show only selected visible conduct and cannot reveal sealed material, unfiled work, confidential settlements, abandoned strategies, or internal deliberation.

asset_planes.name: Economic and governance control plane
asset_planes.definition: Ownership, professional independence, policy authority, vendor rights, system custody, retention, deletion, audit, pricing, and capital-allocation decisions.
asset_planes.boundary: Ownership or partnership never implies unrestricted access, privilege waiver, secondary-use authority, or permission to direct professional judgment.

sensitivity_classes.name: Intentionally public
sensitivity_classes.release_rule: May be used only with public-source provenance and an incompleteness statement.

sensitivity_classes.name: Firm-internal operations
sensitivity_classes.release_rule: Keep firm-controlled unless a named owner approves a bounded public abstraction.

sensitivity_classes.name: Client-related metadata or personnel data
sensitivity_classes.release_rule: Do not publish or externalize by default; combinations may re-identify people and matters.

sensitivity_classes.name: Analysis, strategy, advice, or work product
sensitivity_classes.release_rule: Treat as highly restricted and synthetic-only in this repository.

sensitivity_classes.name: Crown-jewel or regulated material
sensitivity_classes.release_rule: Exclude from this repository, including credentials, trust funds, sealed data, conflicts details, and consequential strategic material.

stages.name: Market intelligence, business development, and referrals
stages.scope: Prospects, panels, referrals, pitches, relationship ownership, rate proposals, and market positioning before a specific engagement.
does_not_prove: A pitch theme does not prove the firm's internal legal strategy or a provider's intent.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Privacy, solicitation, and professional-conduct requirements vary by jurisdiction.

stages.name: Prospective-client contact and pre-intake
stages.scope: Initial contact, problem summary, adverse parties, urgency, jurisdiction, and routing before a representation is accepted.
does_not_prove: An initial contact does not prove that a representation formed or that the preliminary classification was correct.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Prospective-client duties and formation of the relationship are fact- and jurisdiction-dependent.

stages.name: Conflicts and eligibility clearance
stages.scope: Entity and affiliate matching, adversity analysis, waivers, screens, ethical walls, and clearance decisions.
does_not_prove: A name match does not prove a conflict, and a cleared matter does not prove the absence of every conflict.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Conflict, waiver, and screening rules vary by jurisdiction and matter type.

stages.name: Client acceptance and enterprise risk
stages.scope: Accept-or-decline decisions, competence and capacity checks, sanctions or KYC review, credit, insurer, reputational, and strategic fit.
defensive_controls: separate compliance facts from strategic rationale
does_not_prove: A decline category does not establish the full reason, legal merit, or a firm's general policy.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Sanctions, KYC, professional competence, and insurer requirements depend on governing rules and client facts.

stages.name: Engagement, scope, pricing, and client instructions
stages.scope: Engagement terms, scope and exclusions, outside-counsel rules, budgets, fees, assumptions, and client instructions including approved AI use.
defensive_controls: convert client instructions into enforceable matter policy
does_not_prove: A general engagement clause does not prove informed consent for every later tool, data class, or purpose.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Fee, communication, consent, and engagement requirements vary by jurisdiction and client agreement.

stages.name: Matter opening and information governance
stages.scope: Matter identity, taxonomy, repositories, team, residency, retention, legal holds, access groups, ethical walls, and connector policies.
does_not_prove: A matter taxonomy label does not establish the client's full objectives or the controlling legal analysis.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Retention, hold, privacy, residency, and professional obligations depend on matter and jurisdiction.

stages.name: Staffing, resourcing, budget, and workplan
stages.scope: Work breakdown, roles, skills, staffing pyramid, supervision, timeline, workload, budget, and internal handoffs.
defensive_controls: separate workforce analytics from model improvement
does_not_prove: An assignment does not prove expertise, performance, authorship, or responsibility for the final result.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Supervision, staffing, labor, privacy, and fee rules vary by jurisdiction and engagement.

stages.name: Fact development, evidence, diligence, and discovery
stages.scope: Chronologies, custodians, evidence graphs, collections, diligence and discovery plans, review tags, interviews, and investigations.
does_not_prove: A review tag does not prove admissibility, privilege, credibility, or the truth of a fact.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Discovery, diligence, evidence, privacy, privilege, and work-product rules are matter-specific.

stages.name: Legal research and analysis
stages.scope: Issue trees, authorities, citator status, jurisdictional rules, research queries, memoranda, analogies, counterarguments, and uncertainty.
defensive_controls: separate public-corpus research from matter context
does_not_prove: A research query does not prove the firm's position, the client's objective, or the final advice.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Controlling authority, citation duties, confidentiality, and work product vary by forum and matter.

stages.name: Strategy, options, exposure, and matter economics
stages.scope: Goals, scenario trees, alternatives, risk assessments, settlement or deal ranges, expected reactions, exposure, and strategic plans.
defensive_controls: ban secondary use and cross-client derivation
does_not_prove: A modeled scenario does not prove intent, legal merit, authority to settle, or the eventual course of action.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Authority, privilege, settlement, fiduciary, and professional duties depend on client and matter.

stages.name: Drafting, review, and redlining
stages.scope: Drafts, comments, diffs, clause or argument libraries, fallbacks, approvals, and iterative review of legal work product.
does_not_prove: An edit does not prove why it was made, whether it is correct, or whether it represents general firm policy.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Privilege, work product, authorship, supervision, and filing duties depend on matter and forum.

stages.name: Negotiation, advocacy, filings, hearings, and transaction execution
stages.scope: Offers, counteroffers, preparation, depositions, filings, hearings, negotiation moves, closing checklists, and execution steps.
does_not_prove: A public filing or executed term does not reveal confidential advice, rejected alternatives, internal disagreements, or full client objectives.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Procedure, evidence, ethics, negotiation, filing, and transaction requirements vary by forum and deal.

stages.name: Supervision, quality assurance, privilege review, ethics, and candor
stages.scope: Review assignments, signoffs, citation and fact checks, privilege review, validation, ethical escalation, overrides, and incident handling.
does_not_prove: An override or error does not by itself establish negligence, incompetence, intent, or systemic policy.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Supervision, candor, privilege, competence, and professional responsibility rules vary by jurisdiction.

stages.name: Client communication, advice, instruction, and decision
stages.scope: Status, advice, options, consent, client instructions, recommendations, decisions, and secure communication throughout the matter.
defensive_controls: prevent communications from becoming provider telemetry
does_not_prove: Communication metadata does not prove the substance, adequacy, or acceptance of legal advice.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Communication, authority allocation, informed consent, and confidentiality duties are jurisdiction- and matter-dependent.

stages.name: Resolution, settlement, judgment, closing, and remedies
stages.scope: Settlement, judgment, remedies, closing conditions, executed documents, closing books, obligation schedules, and residual risk.
defensive_controls: separate public artifact from private genealogy
does_not_prove: An outcome does not prove causation, strategy quality, client satisfaction, or the hidden alternatives considered.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Settlement, judgment, enforcement, closing, confidentiality, and client-authority rules depend on matter and forum.

stages.name: Timekeeping, work in progress, and pricing variance
stages.scope: Time entries, narratives, task codes, write-downs, budgets, variance, profitability, rework, and task-level economics.
defensive_controls: never send raw narratives by default
does_not_prove: Time, tokens, or cost do not prove legal complexity, work quality, value, or substantive strategy.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Fee, billing, labor, privacy, and client-instruction requirements vary by jurisdiction and agreement.

stages.name: Billing and e-billing
stages.scope: Invoices, billing narratives, task codes, outside-counsel-rule compliance, rejections, disputes, write-offs, and adjustments.
defensive_controls: prohibit invoice text training or secondary use
does_not_prove: A billing rejection does not prove that work lacked value, was unnecessary, or was performed incorrectly.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Fee reasonableness, client guidelines, tax, and billing rules vary by engagement and jurisdiction.

stages.name: Collections, cash application, trust accounting, and disbursement
stages.scope: Accounts receivable, payment promises, receipts, remittances, fee disputes, client trust ledgers, disbursements, and cash application.
defensive_controls: exclude credentials and payment rails from generative AI
does_not_prove: Payment timing or an aging category does not prove client solvency, satisfaction, or the merits of a fee dispute.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Trust accounting, fee disputes, collections, banking, and client-property rules are jurisdiction-specific.

stages.name: Matter closure, retention, return, destruction, and relationship follow-up
stages.scope: Closing memos, final disposition, return or destruction, legal holds, access deprovisioning, conflict updates, client surveys, and follow-up.
does_not_prove: A closed docket or matter status does not prove complete deletion, resolved risk, or client authorization for reuse.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Retention, destruction, client file, hold, and former-client duties vary by jurisdiction and agreement.

stages.name: Knowledge capture, precedent, debrief, and reuse
stages.scope: Playbooks, clause and argument banks, checklists, taxonomies, deviations, rationales, debriefs, lessons, precedents, and reuse governance.

artifacts.name: debrief, exception rationale, and outcome linkage

does_not_prove: A repeated deviation does not prove a general rule, causal success, provider retention, or authority for cross-client reuse.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Privilege, confidentiality, work product, ownership, client consent, and reuse rules depend on source matter and jurisdiction.

stages.name: Talent, training, apprenticeship, evaluation, and succession
stages.scope: Competency maps, assignments, review comments, training, mentoring, performance, promotion, succession, and communities of expertise.
does_not_prove: A staffing or review graph does not prove individual competence, future performance, or ownership of institutional knowledge.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Employment, privacy, discrimination, professional supervision, and retention rules vary by jurisdiction.

stages.name: Vendors, technology, data, AI, cybersecurity, and records
stages.scope: Vendor inventory, data flows, model and connector selection, retention and deletion, security assessments, incidents, audits, and portability.
does_not_prove: A contract promise does not prove runtime behavior, and service processing does not prove training or secondary use.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Vendor, privacy, security, breach, confidentiality, and professional duties depend on deployment and jurisdiction.

stages.name: Firm governance, professional independence, quality, risk, and economics
stages.scope: Policies, committees, compensation, capital allocation, practice strategy, insurance, quality, ownership, independence, and enterprise risk.

artifacts.name: firm risk appetite and strategic plan

does_not_prove: Ownership, investment, partnership, or acquisition does not imply authority to access client information, waive privilege, or direct legal judgment.
stages.jurisdiction_dependency: Ownership and professional-independence regimes vary materially, including regulated alternatives in some jurisdictions.
